title: Recipe Blog CSS and Image Management

content.problemStatement:
In this practice problem, you'll add images to the HTML based on an existing Python dictionary in `main.py`.

In `main.py` - there is dictionary called `recipes` which has the title, description and image for 2 separate recipes. \
In `base.html` - style.css has already been included.

### Task
You need to make changes only in the `index.html` file.

Using Jinja2 templating, do the following
- Add an if condition to check the recipe dictionary is empty or not
- Within the if condition 
   - Iterate through the `recipe` dictionary to load content of the recipe including the image
   - **Note:** `{{ url_for('static', filename='images/' + recipe.image) }}` - This is used inside an HTML template in a Flask app to dynamically generate the URL of an image stored in the static/images/ folder based on the dictionary value - `recipe.image`

### Expected output
When you run the code - the HTML should load with the title, description and image for 2 separate recipes as shown below.
